title: FIGS Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
text: FIGS reported Q1 2026 net revenues of $159.9 million, up 28% year-over-year, exceeding guidance of low 20% growth. Scrubwear revenue reached $126.3 million (up 27%, 79% of total) while non-scrubwear revenue hit $33.6 million (up 31%, strongest in 3 years). Active customers surpassed 3 million for the first time, up 12% YoY. Average order value increased to $124, up 4%. Trailing 12-month revenue per active customer was $220, up 6%. Net income reached $6.3 million vs. $100K loss prior year. Diluted EPS was $0.03 vs. breakeven. Adjusted EBITDA margin was 8.7%, exceeding guidance by 170 basis points. Operating margin was 2.8% vs. -0.2% loss. U.S. revenue grew 24% to $131.6 million while international revenue surged 50% to $28.3 million, now in 85 markets vs. 32 at end of 2024. Full-year 2026 guidance was raised with net revenue growth of 14-16% (previously 10-12%), operating margin of 7.8-8.0% (previously 7.6-7.9%), and adjusted EBITDA margin of 13.0-13.2% (previously 12.7-12.9%). Q2 outlook calls for low 20% revenue growth. Product innovation included FORMx fabric adoption nearly doubling YoY, FIBREx fabrication expanding beyond Olympics launch, maternity styles reintroduced after 3-year gap, and strong performance in wider-leg pant silhouettes. Marketing campaigns including 'Never Change' drove double engagement vs. prior year. Four community hubs are planned for H2 2026, doubling existing fleet. Management flagged headwinds including higher inbound freight from surging oil prices, duty drawback program pause, and variable tariff environment. Gross margin expected to improve modestly from prior year's 66.5%, despite tariffs and freight offsetting pricing gains.
